title: Typography
description: Font sizing, line-height, and readable text.

questions.prompt: Recommended body line-height for readability is roughly:
questions.explanation: 1.5-1.7 is the sweet spot for paragraphs. Headings can be tighter.

questions.prompt: Unit that respects user font-size preferences:
questions.explanation: rem = root em. If the user boosts their default size, everything scales.
em is relative to the parent's font-size: 0.5 * 20 = 10px.

questions.prompt: Which pair produces a fluid font size?
questions.explanation: clamp(min, ideal, max) makes text scale within safe bounds.
System stacks let each OS pick its native UI font — fast and familiar.

questions.prompt: font-display: swap tells the browser to:
options: Wait for the custom font to load
Show fallback text immediately, swap when the font arrives
questions.explanation: swap prevents invisible text (FOIT) while custom fonts load.
